sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id UInt32, value Float64, str_value String, created_at DateTime);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 1.0, 'NaN'), (2, 2.0, 'not a number'), (3, NaN, '3.0'), (4, 4.5, NULL), (5, 5.0, '5.0');
SELECT id, value, isNaN(value) AS is_nan_value FROM test_table WHERE value > 0.0;
SELECT id, value, isNaN(value) AS is_nan_value FROM test_table WHERE (id, created_at) IN (SELECT id, created_at FROM test_table WHERE str_value = 'NaN');
SELECT id, value, isNaN(value) AS is_nan_value, (id, value) FROM test_table GROUP BY id, is_nan_value HAVING is_nan_value = 1;
SELECT id, (value, isNaN(value)) FROM (SELECT id, value FROM test_table WHERE str_value IS NOT NULL) AS subquery HAVING isNaN(value) = 1;
INSERT INTO test_table SELECT id + 1, value, str_value FROM test_table WHERE isNaN(value) = 1;
SELECT t1.id, isNaN(t1.value) AS is_nan_value, (t1.value, t2.value) FROM test_table AS t1 JOIN test_table AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.id + 1 WHERE isNaN(t2.value) = 0;
SELECT id, value, str_value, (SELECT isNaN(value) FROM test_table WHERE id = 1) AS nested_is_nan FROM test_table ORDER BY nested_is_nan DESC;
SELECT count() AS nan_count FROM test_table WHERE isNaN(value) = 1;
